summary: George Russell fended off Max Verstappen in a tense battle to win the Austrian Grand Prix, securing Mercedes' second victory of the season after qualifying on a controversial final-lap pole position. The Mercedes driver held his nerve through intense pressure from the Red Bull challenger, delivering a composed drive that visibly relieved team principal Toto Wolff and the Silver Arrows camp. Russell's win moves Mercedes closer in the constructors' fight while demonstrating the team's resurgence at a track where Red Bull typically dominates.
